{{Quote|There are millions of Stennes. We are near-humans.|Stennes Shifter Trinto Duaba, discussing the Stennes|<ref name="SWAJ12" />}}
'''Stennes''' were a [[Near-Human]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stennaros/Legends|Stennaros]]. A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] offshoot of their species, the [[Stennes Shifter/Legends|Stennes Shifters]], were subject to [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] of persecution that led to many Stennes also being [[Death/Legends|killed]]. As a result, the Stennes also persecuted the Shifters, and were a [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] species that avoided outside contact, but were socially and culturally advanced within their native [[Stennaros system/Legends|Stennaros system]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|The standard Stennes shows no exotic physiological developments that would suggest these [Force] abilities.|Imperial sentientologist Obo Rin's report discussing the Stennes Shifter species's Force abilities, with no evidence of the Stennes species sharing the abilities|<ref name="SWAJ12" />}}
[[File:Trinto-duaba.jpg|thumb|A Stennes Shifter, Trinto Duaba, whose species the Stennes looked physically identical to]]
The Stennes were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Mammal/Legends|mammalian]], [[Near-Human]]s hailing from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stennaros/Legends|Stennaros]]. To some [[Human/Legends|Humans]], the Stennes looked sad, sullen, and [[Ghost (folklore)/Legends|ghostly]]. A genetic offshoot for the Stennes, the [[Stennes Shifter/Legends|Stennes Shifters]], were a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] race of [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifters]]. The Stennes were not known to have any physiologic developments suggesting they had the Shifters' abilities.<ref name="SWAJ12" />

The Stennes Shifters looked similar enough to the Stennes that the former was capable of blending in with the latter. Shifters were slight [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoids]] who stood between 1.3 and 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]], had [[Bone/Legends|skull]]-like faces—[[Eye/Legends|eyes]] recessed into the skull and rimmed by dark circles, and [[Color/Legends|grayish]] skin. Otherwise, the Shifters had similar appearances to Humans.<ref name="SWAJ12" /> At least one Shifter had black eyes,<ref name="CSWE I TD">{{CSWECite|I|212–213|Trinto Duaba}}</ref> yellowish blotches and dark, purplish veins marbling the skin.<ref name="Hasbro">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|pack=#37: Trinto Duaba & Dice Ibegon|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TLC37trintodice.asp}}</ref> Among the Stennes and Shifters, another had tan skin and brown hair, including a mustache.<ref name="SWEIV">{{Film|IV}}</ref>

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|While the Stennes avoid contact with the outside world, they are generally sociable and culturally advanced in their own closed system.|Obo Rin, on the Stennes species' society|<ref name="SWAJ12" />}}
The Stennes species was [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] and avoided contact with the outside world, although they were socially and culturally advanced within their native [[Stennaros system/Legends|Stennaros system]]. They further persecuted and hunted down the Shifters, and by a certain [[Time/Legends|time]], the Shifter [[Trinto Duaba/Legends|Trinto Duaba]] mentioned there were millions of Stennes, and a rare few Shifters. The remaining Stennes Shifters were a homeless species not even welcome on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], who would attempt to blend in with the normal Stennes.<ref name="SWAJ12" />

==History==
{{Quote|The Stennes have taken to hunting their own kind, tracking down any shifters that may attempt to conceal themselves among the population.|The Stennes discriminating against the Stennes Shifters|<ref name="SWAJ12" />}}
Many Stennes were [[Death/Legends|killed]] as a result of [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] of persecution against the Stennes Shifters. Such incidents led to the Stennes being xenophobic to the outside world, and also persecuting the Shifters. During the Galactic Civil War, Trinto Duaba discussed [[Gender/Legends|his]] species, along with the baseline Stennes and their homeworld, with [[Shi'ido]] [[Senior Anthropologist]] [[Vandolae]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[sentientologist]] [[Obo Rin]] obtained the information about Stennaros and its Stennes and Stennes Shifter species from Vandolae, which Rin published in his ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]''.<ref name="SWAJ12" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Stennes species was introduced in the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article "[[Shape-shifters]]," written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] for [[February]] [[1997]]'s [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 12|twelfth issue]] of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine.<ref name="SWAJ12" />
